itemReviewed.name: New Era School - Nehru Nagar III,Nehru Nagar

address.streetAddress: 345, Pandav Nagar, Ghaziabad, Uttar Pradesh 201002, India

itemListElement.reviewBody: Pathetic experience with this school. Someone in my known took their child for admission. At that time they said that they would teach him. He has special needs and they were already informed about it. First of all they teach nothing. The teacher told other kids to do his work.  they are saying that they cannot teach him because he has special needs and now they are saying that if we provide them with a shadow teacher then they will consider promoting him. It does not make any sense if we would need to give full fees to the counsellor then what is the use of giving them fees? PATHETIC EXPERIENCE. They also promised that they will improve him in 1 year but it has been 2 years and we still haven't seen any changes but instead we have been seeing bad changes in him like he has started saying bad words and he is only in 2nd grade that means that the teachers do not care what the students are doing

itemReviewed.name: New Era School - Nehru Nagar III,Nehru Nagar

itemListElement.reviewBody:
School is normal but Principal Ms Kiran Dilwal and Admin Mr Ashish Malik are very bad, they don't know how to talk parents. They do mental harressment of parents on name of their child, I don't know why management is not taking action against them. City Branch is very good in every way but main branch Principal Cordinator and Fee Incharge are rude person. Parents par chikhna chillana n unko extra money k liye mentally pressurise krna. Mera bachha is schl m last 8 yrs s tha but is yr isko mentally harras kia n even hme bhi being parent. I know they will disapper my opinion & review from website but everybody knows about them on every social media platform.
They three persons are spoiling my child carrier after taking whole year fee they are saying my child is dropout in October while he went school trip in November even Principal threat me that she will spoil my child carrier if I will complainy against school or her, I tried to talk management but they also didn't meet, they are doing only business on name of education.
Request to each parent if u r planning to get ur child in new era then you can for city branch but pandav nagar branch not gud

itemReviewed.name: New Era School - Nehru Nagar III,Nehru Nagar

itemListElement.reviewBody: This school provide good education.but poor in fees structure. Always seize the chance to take money from parents by saying penalty.. they are putting useless penalty to childrens... I ain't be happy from this school becoz of fee matter and penalty issues.even principal behaviour is soo rude for parents.

itemReviewed.name: New Era School - Nehru Nagar III,Nehru Nagar

itemListElement.reviewBody:
Spent 15 years of my schooling with New Era, Wonderful experience, Supporting teachers☺️, awesome infrastructure and Happy to be the part of New Era...♥️♥️♥️
A place where you get very friendly environment, you can learn many things, even other than studies also. They focus on the curriculum part of the student very much.

itemReviewed.name: New Era School - Nehru Nagar III,Nehru Nagar

itemListElement.reviewBody:
One of the best schools in this locality ,my son learns a lot while enjoying the experience. Would definitely recommend the school.
Also the infrastructure and facilities are very we'll maintained and the environment is extremely clean and positive.
It's a well deserved 5 star.

itemReviewed.name: New Era School - Nehru Nagar III,Nehru Nagar

itemListElement.reviewBody:
The college also aims at achieving a leading role with globally recognized standards and generate professionals with strong leadership & entrepreneurship skills.

The location is not that much good. Because of industrial area, but well maintained by college Organization.

There is two different colleges one is for school block and second one for polytechnic department. Both college have different building infrastructure.

Only 1 km away from the Asia Colombia hospital.
